qa_pairs.question: How can I view all my jobs in the queue using Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: To get an overview of all your jobs in the queue, navigate to the 'Jobs' tab and select 'Active Jobs' in the job monitoring interface.

qa_pairs.question: How do I filter jobs in the job monitoring interface?
qa_pairs.answer: You can filter jobs by using the 'Filter' text box located at the top right of the job monitoring interface.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to sort jobs by status in the job monitoring interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, columns can be sorted by clicking on the column headers, allowing you to sort by job status (running, completed, failed, etc.).

qa_pairs.question: How can I view more details about a specific job in the queue?
qa_pairs.answer: Clicking on '>' to the left of a job will show you more details, such as the start/end time, node list, and account charged.

qa_pairs.question: How can I display all jobs in the queue, not just active ones?
qa_pairs.answer: To show all jobs in the queue, click on the drop-down menu at the top right of the job monitoring interface and select 'All Jobs'.

qa_pairs.question: How do I access interactive applications through Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: To access interactive applications, navigate to the 'Interactive Apps' tab and select the desired application from the drop-down menu.

qa_pairs.question: What job parameters can I choose for an interactive application in Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: You can choose job parameters such as the length of the job in hours, the number of cores, the amount of memory to allocate (in GB), and whether to be notified by email when the job starts.

qa_pairs.question: How do I submit an interactive job once I've selected its parameters?
qa_pairs.answer: After choosing your job parameters, click on the 'Launch' button to submit your job to the queue.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I see the status of my interactive job after submission?
qa_pairs.answer: You will be taken to the 'My Interactive Sessions' page, where you can see the status of your job (e.g., queued, running, or completed).

qa_pairs.question: How do I connect to and launch an interactive application once it's running?
qa_pairs.answer: Once the job has been assigned a node and is running, click on the 'Connect to ...' button, and the application will open in a new tab in your browser.

qa_pairs.question: How can I get terminal access to the node where an interactive application is running?
qa_pairs.answer: To get terminal access to the node, click on the button beside 'Host' starting with '>_'. This will open a terminal window in your browser.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I need to cancel an interactive Open OnDemand job?
qa_pairs.answer: You can kill the job by clicking on the red 'Cancel' button in the job panel on the 'My Interactive Sessions' page.

qa_pairs.question: How do I access a web-based terminal to interact with the cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Navigate to the 'Clusters' tab and select 'Cluster_Name Shell Access'. This will open a new browser tab with a terminal window.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find logs to debug errors for an interactive Open OnDemand job?
qa_pairs.answer: Navigate to the 'My Interactive Sessions' tab, find your active session, and click on the 'output.log' link to open a separate tab displaying the job's output.

qa_pairs.question: What information does the 'output.log' file contain for an interactive job?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'output.log' file contains the standard output and error messages generated by the job, which can help identify issues.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I include when submitting a ticket to SciNet support for an interactive Open OnDemand job error?
qa_pairs.answer: When submitting a ticket to SciNet support, include the 'output.log' file, your 'Session ID' (e.g., '8feb45fa-bc65-4846-8398-2a73c1bf8e5a'), and any other relevant information.
